title: High petrol prices hit consumers twice as businesses pass on cost of fuel surcharges | The Business
description: Consumers are being hit harder in the pocket as companies hit customers with fuel surcharges.  A growing number of businesses are warning they'll have to pass on costs as deisel and petrol prices hit record levels.  AMP Deputy chief economist Diana Mousina says expect broader increase to prices in the next few weeks. "We've seen some transport companies, like the airlines as well as Uber, announce that they're going to be increasing prices", she says. There are predictions this will lead to higher inflation, as supply chains from food to transport are challenged.   Higher inflation is also posing the risk of more rate hikes - amid predictions of the rising threat of a recession.  #ABCBusines Subscribe: http://ab.co/1svxLVE  ABC NEWS provides around the clock coverage of news events as they break in Australia and abroad. title: How secure is Australia's fuel supply? | The Business | ABC NEWS
description: Australia has enough fuel … for now. But there are big questions about our fuel security in the coming months.   The conflict in the Middle East has taken out about one in five barrels of oil from global supply, and the longer that continues the more expensive things get.   However, Australia produces far more than LNG — liquified natural gas — than it uses, and some of the countries that send fuel to Australia need our LNG. So there could be some hard bargains to be driven.   ABC NEWS provides around the clock coverage of news events as they break in Australia and abroad. title: Crane collapses in Dubbo CBD during storm | ABC NEWS
description: Emergency evacuations are under way in the western NSW town of Dubbo after a crane collapsed during a powerful storm.  The crane, on Blight Street in Dubbo's CBD, was buffeted by 115km/h winds just before 4pm.  A video recorded from the window of a nearby building shows the crane rocking in the wind.  The boom then comes loose and collapses into a nearby apartment building on Church Street.  NSW Police have set-up a 150-metre exclusion zone around the crane.  There have been no reports of injuries at this stage.  ABC NEWS provides around the clock coverage of news events as they break in Australia and abroad. title: Iran war could see record EV sales in Australia, says EV expert | The Business | ABC NEWS
description: The war in the Middle East appears to have reignited interest in electric vehicles, or hybrid options that do not require petrol or diesel. The electric vehicle council says car dealers are fielding a rising number of inquiries about their EV options.  So, could Australia be nearing a "tipping point" when the take-up of EVs heads towards mass public adoption? Founder and editor-in-chief of Renew Economy, Giles Parkinson says the anecdotal evidence suggests there's going to be a big jump in EV sales. "In fact, it could be a record month for EV sales in Australia and it will certainly be a record share of the market," he says. With more choice from EV brands from China, and what he's describved as previously 'overblown' range anxiety waning, Giles Parkinson says people are just walking into EV showrooms and buying a perfectly decent electric SUV for $35,000, $37,000 drive away or even less, which you couldn't do that one or two years ago. "One of the funny things is that, people's hesitancy towards EVs was largely dominated by range anxiety and charger anxiety...it was very much overblown," he says. "Now I think we've seen the same thing with, ironically, fossil fuels, some petrol and diesel drivers are cancelling holiday trips because they don't know that the bowser is going to be open and they're also looking at the costs of that trip now, which have inflated about 50 per cent." #ABCBusiness Subscribe: http://ab.co/1svxLVE  ABC NEWS provides around the clock coverage of news events as they break in Australia and abroad. title: 'High risk' of a protracted bear market as Middle East war drags on, says analyst | The Business
description: Global financial markets have been struggling for direction. Billions of dollars are riding on whether the Middle East war will conclude in the coming weeks or whether it will be a prolonged conflict. A fear is emerging that the United States no longer has the ability to find a so-called "off ramp" to the war. It means the Strait of Hormuz, which facilities the passage of a fifth of the world's oil supply, will remain shut. Senior financial market analyst with Capital.com Kyle Rodda says it means "there's a high risk that we could see this result in a protracted bear market". This would see a fall of at least 20 per cent for global share markets from their February peak.   #ABCBusiness Subscribe: http://ab.co/1svxLVE  ABC NEWS provides around the clock coverage of news events as they break in Australia and abroad. title: Google, Meta facing 'existential threat' after losing landmark addiction case | The World | ABC News
description: A Los Angeles jury has found Google and Meta liable for damages in a landmark social media addiction lawsuit that could influence thousands of cases. The plaintiff in the case, a 20-year-old woman, accused the tech companies of causing harm by deliberately designing addictive platforms and was awarded $4.3 million in damages. Jacob Ward, veteran tech journalist and host of the Rip Current podcast told The World's Girish Sawlani, the giant tech firms could be facing billions of dollars in future liabilities.
